annotations.label: Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.snippet: Given a solid ball in three-dimensional space

annotations.note: The notion of equidecomposability is defined in Mathlib via `Equidecomp`, but the Banach–Tarski theorem itself (that a ball is equidecomposable with two copies of itself) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pea and the Sun paradox (alternative form)

anchor.snippet: given any two "reasonable" solid objects

annotations.note: The strong form of Banach–Tarski for bounded subsets with nonempty interiors is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous motion of pieces (2005)

anchor.snippet: the pieces in the decomposition can be chosen in such a way that they can be moved continuously into place

annotations.note: The Wilson 2005 result on continuous motion of pieces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Locale-theoretic resolution (Leroy–Simpson)

anchor.snippet: the Banach–Tarski paradox does not violate volumes if one works with locales

annotations.note: Locale-theoretic machinery (`Sublocale`) is in Mathlib but the Leroy–Simpson volume result is not.

annotations.label: Strong form of the Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.snippet: Given any two bounded subsets A and B of a Euclidean space in at least three dimensions

annotations.note: The strong form of the paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Doubling a ball via translated copies

anchor.snippet: Now let A be the original ball and B be the union of two translated copies of the original ball

annotations.note: The ball-doubling consequence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Countable analogue in dimensions 1 and 2

anchor.snippet: an analogous statement remains true if countably many subsets are allowed

annotations.note: Countable equidecomposability in low dimensions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tarski's characterization of amenable groups

anchor.snippet: Tarski proved that amenable groups are precisely those for which no paradoxical decompositions exist

annotations.note: Mathlib has Følner-filter amenability (`IsFoelner.amenable`) producing an invariant finitely additive measure, but does not formalize Tarski's characterization via (non)paradoxical decompositions.

annotations.label: Congruent figures under G-action

anchor.snippet: Two geometric figures that can be transformed into each other are called congruent

annotations.note: Congruence under a group action is captured by `MulAction.orbitRel`, but no `Congruent`-named definition matches this exactly.

annotations.label: G-equidecomposable sets

anchor.snippet: Two subsets A and B of X are called G -equidecomposable

annotations.note: `Equidecomp X G` (bijection with finite witness `IsDecompOn`) is the Mathlib definition of G-equidecomposability.

annotations.label: Paradoxical set

anchor.snippet: If a set E has two disjoint subsets A and B such that A and E , as well as B and E , are G -equidecomposable, then E is called paradoxical

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Equidecomp` but no `Paradoxical` predicate built on top of it.

annotations.label: Reformulation of Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.snippet: A three-dimensional Euclidean ball is equidecomposable with two copies of itself

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski paradox in equidecomposability form is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robinson's five-piece sharp result

anchor.snippet: doubling the ball can be accomplished with five pieces, and fewer than five pieces will not suffice

annotations.note: Robinson's five-piece minimum is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong version of the paradox

anchor.snippet: Any two bounded subsets of 3-dimensional Euclidean space with non- empty interiors are equidecomposable

annotations.note: The strong version of the paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Banach's Bernstein–Schroeder generalization

anchor.snippet: a generalization of the Bernstein–Schroeder theorem due to Banach

annotations.note: Mathlib has the cardinal-theoretic Schröder–Bernstein but not Banach's equidecomposability version (TODO comment in `Equidecomp.lean`).

annotations.label: Nonexistence of invariant finitely additive measure

anchor.snippet: it is impossible to find a finitely additive measure

annotations.note: The nonexistence of a rotation-invariant finitely additive normalized measure on bounded subsets of R^n (n≥3) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Converse: paradox-free implies invariant measure

anchor.snippet: nonexistence of paradoxical decompositions of this type implies the existence of a finitely additive invariant measure

annotations.note: Mathlib produces an invariant finitely additive measure from Følner conditions but does not state the converse from non-paradoxicality.

annotations.label: Hausdorff paradox

anchor.snippet: the surface of a unit sphere in space is a disjoint union of three sets

annotations.note: The Hausdorff paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-equidecomposability of nested Euclidean polygons

anchor.section: Connection with earlier work and the role of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: Two Euclidean polygons , one of which strictly contains the other, are not equidecomposable

annotations.note: The classical non-equidecomposability of nested polygons is not formalized.

annotations.label: Morse: polygon result provable in ZF

anchor.section: Connection with earlier work and the role of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: A. P. Morse showed that the statement about Euclidean polygons can be proved in ZF set theory

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize metamathematical results about ZF-provability of polygon equidecomposability.

annotations.label: Cohen: independence of AC from ZF

anchor.section: Connection with earlier work and the role of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: Paul Cohen proved that the axiom of choice is independent from ZF

annotations.note: Independence of AC from ZF is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Banach–Tarski not provable in ZF+DC

anchor.section: Connection with earlier work and the role of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: The Banach–Tarski paradox is not a theorem of ZF , nor of ZF + DC

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the independence of Banach–Tarski from ZF+DC.

annotations.label: Pawlikowski: paradox from ZF + Hahn–Banach

anchor.section: Connection with earlier work and the role of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: Janusz Pawlikowski proved that the Banach–Tarski paradox follows from ZF plus the Hahn–Banach theorem

annotations.note: Pawlikowski's derivation of Banach–Tarski from ZF + Hahn–Banach is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free group F_2 on two generators

anchor.snippet: The free group with two generators a and b consists of all finite strings

annotations.note: `FreeGroup α` defines the free group on a generating type; `FreeGroup (Fin 2)` (or `Bool`) gives F₂.

annotations.label: String reduction in F_2

anchor.snippet: abab −1 a −1 concatenated with abab −1 a yields

annotations.note: `FreeGroup.Red` formalizes word reduction in the free group; the specific worked example is not isolated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Paradoxical decomposition of F_2

anchor.snippet: The group [MATH] can be "paradoxically decomposed" as follows

annotations.note: The standard paradoxical partition of F₂ into S(a), S(a⁻¹), S(b), S(b⁻¹) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Free rotation group H isomorphic to F_2

anchor.snippet: Therefore, the group H is a free group, isomorphic to F

annotations.note: No formalization in Mathlib of a free subgroup of SO(3) on two rotations.

annotations.label: Failure in two dimensions

anchor.snippet: This step cannot be performed in two dimensions since it involves rotations in three dimensions

annotations.note: The fact that SO(2) is abelian (hence amenable, no free F₂) is implicit but the Banach–Tarski-related implication is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quaternion proof of free rotation subgroups

anchor.snippet: An alternative arithmetic proof of the existence of free groups in some special orthogonal groups using integral quaternions

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Quaternion` but no formalization of integral-quaternion free subgroups in SO(n).

annotations.label: Paradoxical decomposition of the sphere S^2

anchor.snippet: the paradoxical decomposition of H yields a paradoxical decomposition of S

annotations.note: The paradoxical decomposition of S² (minus fixed points) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decomposition of solid ball minus center

anchor.snippet: connect every point on S 2 with a half-open segment to the origin

annotations.note: Lifting the S² decomposition to the punctured ball is not formalized.

annotations.label: S^2 − D equidecomposable with S^2

anchor.snippet: S 2 − D is equidecomposable with S

annotations.note: The lemma absorbing the countable fixed-point set is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ball minus a point equidecomposable with ball

anchor.snippet: the ball minus a point is equidecomposable with the ball

annotations.note: The translation/rotation trick for the missing center is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transitivity of equidecomposability

anchor.snippet: Use is made of the fact that if A ~ B and B ~ C , then A ~ C

annotations.note: `Equidecomp.trans` composes two equidecompositions into one, witnessing transitivity.

annotations.label: Five pieces is best possible

anchor.snippet: This gives 5 pieces and is the best possible

annotations.note: Robinson's sharp lower bound of 5 pieces is not formalized.

annotations.label: k copies of a ball in n-space

anchor.section: Obtaining infinitely many balls from one
anchor.snippet: it is possible to obtain k copies of a ball in the Euclidean n -space from one

annotations.note: The k-fold (and countable) generalization is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuum-many-piece partition of S^{n−1}

anchor.section: Obtaining infinitely many balls from one
anchor.snippet: the sphere S n −1 can be partitioned into as many pieces as there are real numbers

annotations.note: The continuum-piece partition result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Von Neumann's paradox in the plane

anchor.section: Von Neumann paradox in the Euclidean plane
anchor.snippet: Any two bounded subsets of the Euclidean plane with non-empty interiors are equidecomposable with respect to the area-preserving affine maps

annotations.note: Von Neumann's planar paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laczkovich's SL(2,R) paradoxical decomposition

anchor.snippet: Miklós Laczkovich proved that such a decomposition exists

annotations.note: Laczkovich's result on SL(2,R)-paradoxes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Satô: four pieces suffice for SA_2

anchor.snippet: Kenzi Satô constructed such a subgroup, confirming that four pieces suffice

annotations.note: Satô's four-piece result for SA₂ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tomkowicz: free piecewise linear group on punctured disk

anchor.snippet: Grzegorz Tomkowicz constructed such a group

annotations.note: Tomkowicz's free piecewise-linear group construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adams–Mycielski fractional parts of S^2

anchor.snippet: the unit sphere S 2 contains a set E that is a half, a third, a fourth

annotations.note: Adams–Mycielski's fractional-parts theorem for S² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tomkowicz: generalization to hyperbolic plane

anchor.snippet: Adams and Mycielski construction can be generalized to obtain a set E of H

annotations.note: The hyperbolic-plane generalization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Banach–Tarski paradox in hyperbolic plane

anchor.snippet: there is a Banach–Tarski paradox in the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski analogue in H² is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mycielski–Wagon Borel paradox in H^2

anchor.snippet: constructed a paradoxical decomposition of the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: The Borel paradoxical decomposition of H² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tomkowicz: free discontinuous subgroup of SA(3,Z)

anchor.snippet: who constructed a free properly discontinuous subgroup

annotations.note: Tomkowicz's construction in SA(3,ℤ) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Countable equidecomposability via Lebesgue pieces

anchor.snippet: they have equal Lebesgue measures if and only if they are countably equidecomposable using Lebesgue measurable pieces

annotations.note: The Lebesgue-measurable countable equidecomposability characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Extension to Lie and locally compact groups

anchor.snippet: extended this result to finite dimensional Lie groups

annotations.note: The Lie-group / locally compact group extension is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coloring rule linking paradoxes and optimization

anchor.snippet: introduced a coloring rule of points in a Cantor space that links paradoxical decompositions with optimization

annotations.note: The Cantor-space coloring-rule link is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genericity of bounded paradoxical sets

anchor.snippet: in the case of non-supramenable connected Lie groups

annotations.note: Genericity of paradoxical bounded sets for non-supramenable Lie groups is not formalized in Mathlib.
